title: West Lake core loop works best in half a day
content: For first-time visitors, West Lake, Broken Bridge, and nearby lakefront walking routes fit better as a half-day block than a full-day marathon. Keep Lingyin Temple on a separate half day to reduce cross-city movement.

title: Hangzhou food planning works better around Hefang Street and Wulin
content: If the trip includes local food, cluster evening food stops around Hefang Street, Hubin, or Wulin so the user can walk between snacks instead of taking repeated taxis after dinner.

title: Hotel choice should favor West Lake east side or metro-connected districts
content: For short city breaks, hotel locations on the east side of West Lake or near major metro transfers reduce repeated detours and keep early-morning departures more stable.

title: Gulangyu and Zhongshan Road should not be squeezed into too many fragments
content: In Xiamen, Gulangyu usually deserves a focused half day or more. Zhongshan Road works best as a same-evening food and walking block instead of a separate commute-heavy day.

title: Museum-heavy Beijing itineraries need reservation and buffer time
content: If the user prefers museums in Beijing, reserve large blocks for each museum cluster and avoid adding far-away evening stops on the same day because security checks and queues add uncertainty.

title: Night views are easier when Bund and nearby districts stay on one day
content: For short Shanghai trips, Bund, Nanjing East Road, and nearby night-view stops should stay on the same day to avoid repeated river-crossing transit at night.
